issue_title: Add preset style selection and preview
issue_body:
Extend the new-episode setup so that after creators assign their speaker tracks and social links, they can choose a polished visual preset for the episode before moving on. The step should present a small set of distinct podcast styles with layout and pacing choices, show a live preview using the assigned speakers, and make the selected style part of the episode setup summary. This keeps the product moving from raw import toward a publishable visual identity without dropping users into a blank canvas too early.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
Running `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes. In the app, a creator can complete the existing episode setup flow, choose among at least three visibly different preset styles, see the preview update when a style is selected, adjust simple layout/pacing options, and see the chosen style, layout, and pacing reflected in the final setup review or generated episode plan.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The first setup flow exists, so the next missing foundation is the preset-driven visual choice promised by the vision. It is more valuable now than building a pro canvas or export path because creators need a guided style decision immediately after import, and that selected style can later drive editing, templates, captions, and export behavior._
